s2.ptxt: Visually check the drive belt for excessive wear, frayed cords, etc.
If any defect is found, replace the drive belt.

atten4.ptxt: Cracks on the rib side of a belt are considered acceptable. Replace the belt if there are any missing ribs.

s2.ptxt: Keep the vehicle level.
With the engine stopped, check the fluid level in the fluid reservoir.
If necessary, add fluid.

specitem.ptxt: ATF DEXRON II or III, or equivalent

atten4.ptxt: If the fluid is hot, check that the fluid level is within the HOT range on the fluid reservoir. If the fluid is cold, check that the fluid level is within the COLD range.

s2.ptxt: Start the engine and run it at idle.
Turn the steering wheel from lock to lock several times to raise fluid temperature.
Check for foaming or emulsification.
If foaming or emulsification is identified, bleed the power steering system (See page ).
With the engine idling, measure the fluid level in the fluid reservoir.
Stop the engine.
Wait a few minutes and remeasure the fluid level in the fluid reservoir.
If a problem is found, bleed the power steering system (See page ).
Check the fluid level.
Disconnect the pressure feed tube assembly from the vane pump assembly (See page for 2AZ-FE, for 2GR-FE).
Connect SST as shown in the illustration.

atten3.ptxt: Check that the valve of the SST is in the open position.

s2.ptxt: Bleed the power steering system (See page ).
Start the engine and run it at idle.
Turn the steering wheel from lock to lock several times to raise fluid temperature.
With the engine idling, close the valve of the SST and observe the reading on the SST.

item.ptxt: Do not keep the valve closed for more than 10 seconds.
Do not allow the fluid temperature to become too high.

s2.ptxt: With the engine idling, open the valve fully.
Measure the fluid pressure at engine speeds of 1,000 rpm and 3,000 rpm.

atten3.ptxt: Do not turn the steering wheel.

s2.ptxt: With the engine idling and the valve fully opened, turn the steering wheel left or right to the full lock position. Observe the reading on the SST.

item.ptxt: Do not keep the steering wheel in the full lock position for more than 10 seconds.
Do not allow the fluid temperature to become too high.

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the SST.
Connect the pressure feed tube assembly to the vane pump assembly (See page for 2AZ-FE, for 2GR-FE).
Bleed the power steering system (See page ).
Stop the vehicle on a level, paved surface and turn the wheels straight ahead.
Disconnect the negative (-) battery cable from the battery (See page ).
Remove the steering pad (See page ).
Connect the negative (-) battery cable to the battery.
Using a torque wrench, check if the steering wheel set nut is properly tightened.
Start the engine and run it at idle.
Turn the steering wheel 90 degrees to the right and check steering effort (torque) while turning the wheel. Check the opposite direction in the same manner.

atten4.ptxt: Check tire type, pressure, and the road surface before making your diagnosis.

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the negative (-) battery cable from the battery.
Torque the steering wheel set nut.
Install the steering pad (See page ).
Connect the negative (-) battery cable to the battery.
Clear the DTCs (See page ).
Inspect the airbag warning light (See page ).
